target_text: I stay here. Tell me how experiments go.

input_text: question: Well, how do I do it? I don’t understand xenonite.
target_text: Rotate tunnel.

input_text: question: The generator isn’t broken, If there are Taumoeba in the generator, it means there are Taumoeba in the fuel supply. Taumoeba ate all the Astrophage. We have no power because we have no fuel.
target_text: How Taumoeba get into fuel, question?!

input_text: question: Is that blocking your radiator? Isn’t that dangerous?
target_text: No. This make hot air into cold air, I test. Works! Happy!

input_text: question: ...Okay.
target_text: I thought you made peace, question?

input_text: question: Nothing—wait…I see something…. Okay, how do I use the batteries…?

input_text: question: The Taumoeba evolved to resist nitrogen. But it also evolved to get into xenonite to hide from nitrogen. The side effect is Taumoeba-82.5 can work its way through xenonite over time.
target_text: Amaze. Now what, question?

input_text: question: Wait. I have more. Much more.
target_text: More, question? Good.

input_text: question: Yes. Now. Prepare for gravity.
target_text: Yes, gravity. For science gear.

input_text: question: We’ll create the needed rotation first, then shut off beetles, then unspool cables.
target_text: If ship not unspooled, force is too much for human.

input_text: question: Yeah, but no oxygen. This is space. How did it burn?
target_text: Theory: Many Astrophage in tanks. Some probably dead. Dead Astrophage have water. Dead Astrophage not immune to heat. Water and much much heat become hydrogen and oxygen. Oxygen and heat and hull becomes burn marks.

input_text: question: I’m going to put one on each beetle. Just in case something happens to the Hail Mary on the way home.
target_text: Good plan! You are smart! I can make these for you. Also, today I finish fuel-transfer device. Can give you Astrophage now. Then we both go home!

input_text: question: Can we do all that within fifty Earth years?
target_text: Probably not. Why so fast, question?

input_text: question: What? He tells me what to do, he tells me why to do it, he tells me how to do it, he tells me when to do it, and then when I do it, he’s like, “what are you doing?” I’m just saying you’re gonna get out of the ball and get into a much bigger ball?
target_text: Yes, for sleeping

input_text: question: It’s just carbon dioxide and nitrogen! Only carbon dioxide and nitrogen! Nothing more! Don’t ask me again!
target_text: Yes. No ask again. Sorry.

input_text: question: Happy.
target_text: Sample device radio signal strong, Getting closer. Be ready.

target_text: Can hear. Who are you talking to?

input_text: question: So?
target_text: Most life no can live outside its air. I die if not in Erid air. You die if not in Earth air. But Taumoeba survive when not in Adrian air. Taumoeba stronger than Erid life—stronger than Earth life.

input_text: question: Fix. You fix. Wait, I sleep now,

input_text: question: Agreed.
target_text: Good findings,

target_text: My Astrophage in sealed xenonite container. Is safe. You sleep now.

input_text: question: That wouldn’t power my spin drive for long. Thirty seconds or so. And it probably wouldn’t live long enough. There’s Taumoeba everywhere on my side of the partition. Keep your Astrophage safe on your side.
target_text: I make new engine, Taumoeba turn Astrophage into methane. React with oxygen. Make fire. Make thrust. Get to my ship. Much Astrophage there.

input_text: question: I made Venus in the tank. But not quite Venus. Venus’s air is 96.5 percent carbon dioxide and 3.5 percent nitrogen. I started with just the carbon dioxide. The Taumoeba were fine. Then I added the nitrogen. And the Taumoeba all died.
target_text: All die, question? Sudden, question?

input_text: question: It’s fine, it’s just—the sky is...slightly on fire.
target_text: Words of encouragement

input_text: question: You’re the only person on that huge ship?!
target_text: Original crew was twenty-three. Now is only me.

input_text: question: We’re dead in space. We’re stuck here forever.
target_text: Not forever,

input_text: question: Okay. What will you use that for?
target_text: If all plans work, we make good Taumoeba. I give you fuel. You go Earth and I go Erid. We say goodbye.
